title: Contributing to easyRasch2
text: Contributions, bug reports, questions welcome.

title: Contributing to easyRasch2
text: Please report bugs unexpected behaviour issue tracker. good report includes: minimal reproducible example (e.g. using built-dataset bundled phq9 data eRm::pcmdat2), output sessionInfo(), installed version (packageVersion("easyRasch2")). Searching existing issues first helps avoid duplicates.

title: Contributing to easyRasch2
text: Open issue describing change starting substantial work, scope approach can discussed. Fork repository create feature branch. Follow existing code style package’s RM-prefixed naming conventions. Add update tests tests/testthat/ run devtools::test() devtools::check() clean. Update roxygen2 documentation , relevant, NEWS.md. Open pull request referencing issue.

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: vignette walks steps Rasch analysis using data nine-item Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9; Kroenke et al. (2001)). example follows four psychometric criteria proposed Christensen et al. (2021) validation patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs): Unidimensionality — items measure single latent construct. Local independence — conditioning latent trait, item responses independent . Ordered response category thresholds (monotonicity) — moving latent trait increases probability higher categories. Invariance / DIF — item parameters across relevant external groups (e.g. gender). move complementary descriptors commonly reported alongside four criteria : Targeting — well person item locations overlap latent continuum. Reliability — precisely scale separates respondents. Person fit — unexpected response patterns. Item person parameters — estimates reuse scoring. extensive treatment Rasch analysis R, see https://pgmj.github.io/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.html. Bayesian sibling package, see https://pgmj.github.io/easyRaschBayes/. NOTE: simulation-based functions use low number iterations make vignette render faster. use iterations actual analysis work. methods, 500-1500 useful, except conditional infit, lower numbers can optimal, depending sample size (Johansson 2025). plan use p_value = TRUE, use least 1000 iterations. get faster simulations, please make use options(mc.cores = 4), 4 replaced number high performance CPU cores computer available. setting automatically applied functions can use parallel processing.

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: bundled phq9 dataset 600-respondent random subsample PHQ-9 module U.S. National Health Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES, September 2024 release) complete responses nine items. NHANES microdata released public domain U.S. federal government.
code: library(easyRasch2) data(phq9) items <- phq9[, 1:9]   # 9 item columns, scored 0..3  # add item information item_desc <- c(   "Little interest or pleasure in doing things",   "Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless",   "Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much",   "Feeling tired or having little energy",   "Poor appetite or overeating",   "Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down",   "Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television",   "Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed",   "Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way" )  item_resp <- c("Not at all","Several days","More than \nhalf the days","Nearly every day") str(items) #> 'data.frame':    600 obs. of  9 variables: #>  $ q1: int  3 0 1 2 3 3 1 3 2 1 ... #>  $ q2: int  3 0 2 3 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q3: int  3 1 3 0 3 1 0 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q4: int  3 1 3 2 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q5: int  3 0 3 2 3 2 0 1 2 0 ... #>  $ q6: int  3 2 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q7: int  3 3 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q8: int  1 0 2 0 3 3 0 0 1 0 ... #>  $ q9: int  3 0 0 2 3 1 2 0 0 0 ... summary(rowSums(items)) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    0.00   10.00   16.00   15.41   21.00   27.00 table(phq9$gender, useNA = "ifany") #>  #> Female   Male   <NA>  #>    426    143     31 summary(phq9$age) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   15.00   26.00   33.00   36.06   44.00   85.00

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: easyRasch2 provides several complementary unidimensionality diagnostics can combined robust conclusion: item-level conditional infit MSQ statistics (Müller 2020) item-level item-restscore associations Goodman-Kruskal’s \gamma (gamma) (Kreiner 2011) confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) WLSMV estimator ordinal data principal components analysis (PCA) standardized residuals (Chou Wang 2010) Martin-Löf test Monte-Carlo p-values (Christensen Kreiner 2007)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: important note RMitemInfit() function uses conditional infit, robust different sample sizes makes ZSTD unnecessary (Müller 2020). Müller also questions usefulness outfit, simulation study (Johansson 2025) reached conclusion. Thus, outfit reported. Conditional item infit mean-square statistics flag items whose response patterns deviate Rasch expectation. RMitemInfitCutoff(), per-item highest-density intervals serve reference instead rule--thumb cutoffs (Johansson 2025). Bootstrap p-values also available via p_value = TRUE, family-wise error rate (FWER; default) false discovery rate (FDR) correction. NOTE: functions use simulation-based cutoffs (except RMitemInfitMI()) optional p_value = TRUE table outputs. MSQ values based conditional estimation. n = 600 respondents. Cutoff values based 100 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI). Flagged: overfit = infit range (predictable); underfit = range (noisier). can also get plot summarizing simulated observed item infit, using RMitemInfitPlot(). Since conditional infit needs complete data, sibling functions combine multiple imputation conditional infit useful partial missingness: RMitemInfitMI() RMitemInfitCutoffMI(). Based table, items 3, 8, 9 underfit Rasch model, items 2 4 overfit. Item 2 “Feeling , depressed, hopeless”, general item terms measuring depression, expected. low item fit value, often referred item “overfit” Rasch model, indicates responses may predictable. often case items general/broad scope relation latent variable. often find overfitting items also local dependence (residual correlations) issues items. Overfit may likened much stronger factor loading items confirmatory factor analysis higher level discrimination Item Response Theory model two parameters. high item fit value, often referred “underfit” Rasch model, can indicate several things. Often underfit due multidimensionality question difficult interpret thus noisy response data. latter instance caused question asks two things time, ambiguous reasons.
code: infit_cut <- RMitemInfitCutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                            seed = 3) RMitemInfit(items, cutoff = infit_cut)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Item-restscore uses Goodman-Kruskal’s \gamma (gamma) shows expected observed correlation item score based rest items (Kreiner 2011). Similarly, inverted, item infit, lower observed correlation value expected indicates underfit, item may belong dimension. higher expected observed value indicates overfitting possibly redundant item. Overfitting items often also show issues local dependency. Compared infit, item-restscore often flags overfit items (based experience), less often flags underfit items (based simulation study (Johansson 2025)). Item-restscore associations. n = 600 respondents. Flagged (adj. p < .05): overfit = observed expected (-discrimination, often local dependence); underfit = (-discrimination, often multidimensionality/noise). Similarly infit, item-restscore found items 2 4 overfit 8 underfit. also found item 6 overfit. methods best used together.
code: RMitemRestscore(items)

text: RMdimCFACutoff() simulates data unidimensional PCM fits unidimensional ordinal CFA simulated dataset, building parametric-bootstrap reference distribution model fit indices (SRMR, CFI, RMSEA) per-item standardized factor loadings. returns simulation object; RMdimCFA() fits CFA observed data tabulates observed fit indices loadings simulated reference. Observed values beyond simulated cutoffs implausible unidimensional data-generating process. Partial Credit Model posterior-predictive CFA fit-index check. Observed CFA fit (one-factor, lavaan WLSMV, ordered = TRUE) vs simulated null PCM unidimensionality (100 iterations n = 600 simulees). n = 600 respondents. Cutoffs one-sided 99th percentile; flagged observed value lies worst 1% null unfavourable direction. Standardized factor loadings (one-factor, lavaan WLSMV) vs simulated expected range PCM unidimensionality (100 iterations n = 600 simulees). n = 600 respondents. Expected range two-sided central 99% interval simulated loadings; Flagged = / range. RMdimCFAPlot(cfa_cut, data = items) returns list two figures: $loadings (observed loadings simulated expected ranges) $fit (simulated fit-index distributions observed values overlaid). table agrees mostly earlier findings. overall model fit clearly worse expected unidimensionality item 2 overfit (higher standardized factor loading expected), items 3 8 underfit (lower loadings). Additionally, CFA flags item 1 overfit. however take simulation-based results grain salt since using iterations get reliable results.
code: cfa_cut <- RMdimCFACutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                        seed = 2) cfa_tbl <- RMdimCFA(items, cutoff = cfa_cut) cfa_tbl$fit cfa_tbl$loadings

text: fitting Rasch model, residuals contain systematic structure. largest eigenvalue residual correlation matrix can considered headline diagnostic; value clearly simulation-based cutoff suggests secondary dimension. Standardized loadings first residual contrast

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: likelihood-ratio test unidimensionality priori specified multidimensional alternative. p-value obtained parametric-bootstrap (Monte Carlo) sampling unidimensional null. significant p-value constitutes strong evidence items measure one common latent variable. However, non-significant p-value indicates data consistent one common latent variable across partition, considered proof unidimensionality . use case demonstrated , hypothesized five psychosomatic symptoms separate subscale items. Looking PCA plot , can see tendencies clustering based loadings first residual contrast factor, items 3-5 clearly deviating 1, 2, 6, 9. Using PCA plot determine item partitioning M-L test recommended; see ?RMdimMartinLof details references. p-value rejects unidimensionality across partition, subscale WLE correlation indicates dimensions strongly related. M-L test results can investigated using diagnostic function RMdimMartinLofResiduals().

title: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory
text: Estimation: single engine across package — Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) item parameters via psychotools Warm’s Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters. eRm used Andersen’s LR test (RMdifLR()); mirt (MML) available optional engine (estimator = "MML" RMlocdepQ3() RMitemParameters()) plausible values behind RMU reliability metric. Inference: simulation-based cutoffs throughout, optional bootstrap p-values (p_value = TRUE) using Westfall–Young family-wise correction (default) FDR alternatives (Ferreira, 2024). Output: knitr::kable() tables (Quarto-friendly), ggplot2 figures, "dataframe" output options downstream use. Every caption reports estimation sample size missing-data policy. Naming: Functions use RM prefix (e.g., RMlocdepQ3()).

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMdifGamma
text: Partial gamma (Bjorner et al., 1998) measures association item response exogenous grouping variable, controlling total score. Values near 0 indicate DIF. Recommended interpretive thresholds (Bjorner et al., 1998): negligible DIF: gamma within \([-0.21, 0.21]\), gamma significantly different 0. Slight moderate DIF: gamma within \([-0.31, 0.31]\) (outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\)), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). Moderate large DIF: gamma outside \([-0.31, 0.31]\), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports). Bootstrap p-values. p_value = TRUE, item's observed partial gamma compared simulated null distribution (cutoff$results, DIF variable random construction). per-item statistic residual studentised bootstrap mean SD; marginal p-value two-sided Monte-Carlo p-value (1 + #\{|t*| >= |t|\}) / (B + 1), can smaller 1 / (B + 1). correction = "fwer" uses Westfall-Young studentised-max step-, exploits bootstrap dependence among items (Ferreira, 2024); liberal simulation small, least 1000 iterations RMdifGammaCutoff() recommended (warning issued ). Unlike asymptotic p-values iarm::partgam_DIF(), calibrated simulated Rasch null rather asymptotic SE; model-conditional sample-size-sensitive, reported alongside simulated effect-size band, place .

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMdifGamma
text: marginal p-value controls error rate single comparison: one item (item pair) decided advance relevant value. scanning k comparisons flagging whichever fall alpha tests k hypotheses , chance least one false flag inflates roughly \(1 - (1 - \alpha)^k\) (e.g. 34% k = 8 alpha = 0.05) – even every marginal p-value correctly calibrated. corrected (adjusted) p-value controls : correction = "fwer" bounds probability false flag (strict, lower power), "fdr_bh" / "fdr_by" bound expected proportion false flags among raised (lenient middle ground). Rule thumb: use marginal p-value single pre-specified comparison, corrected p-value screening whole table – usual workflow.

what: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Detects Differential Item Functioning (DIF) recursively splitting sample one covariates using psychotree::raschtree() (dichotomous data) psychotree::pctree() (polytomous data), computes per-split effect-size measures every item – Mantel-Haenszel odds-ratio (Holland & Thayer, 1986), Delta scale developed Educational Testing Service (ETS; Zwick, 2012), dichotomous data, partial gamma coefficient polytomous data – classifies ETS /B/C categories (Bjorner et al., 1998). Optionally, iterative purification step (Henninger et al., 2025) applied, tree stability across resamples can assessed via stablelearner::stabletree() (Philipp et al., 2018).

"auto" uses MH RM partial gamma PCM. purification "none" (default) "iterative". Iterative purification recomputes effect size excluding items already classified DIF matching score (Bjorner et al., 1998). p_adj Multiple-testing adjustment partial-gamma classification: "none" (default), "fdr" (Benjamini & Hochberg), "bonferroni". title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Continuous covariates (e.g., age years) interactions among multiple covariates handled natively model-based recursive-partitioning machinery partykit / psychotree. ETS Delta-scale Mantel-Haenszel. MH common odds ratio \(\hat{\alpha}_{MH}\) mapped ETS Delta scale via \(\Delta_{MH} = -2.35 \log \hat{\alpha}_{MH}\). classification rules (Holland & Thayer, 1988; Zwick, 2012) : Class : \(|\Delta_{MH}| < 1\) test \(\Delta_{MH} = 0\) rejected alpha. Class C: \(|\Delta_{MH}| \ge 1.5\) test \(|\Delta_{MH}| \le 1\) rejected alpha. Class B: otherwise. easyRasch2 uses sign convention effecttree: positive \(\Delta\) indicates item difficult second (reference) group. Partial gamma. iarm::partgam_DIF() provides gamma estimate SE per item. ETS-style classification follows Bjorner et al. (1998): \(|\gamma| < 0.21\) test \(\gamma = 0\) rejected alpha; C \(|\gamma| > 0.31\) test \(|\gamma| \le 0.21\) rejected alpha; B otherwise. Rule--thumb caveat. /B/C boundaries (Delta 1.0 / 1.5; gamma 0.21 / 0.31) conventions carried large-scale educational testing, values calibrated sample items hand – contrast simulation-based cutoffs used elsewhere package – classification best read rough magnitude guide rather calibrated test. sample-calibrated partial-gamma DIF test, see RMdifGamma() RMdifGammaCutoff() object (optionally p_value = TRUE). Continuous interaction effects. recursive partitioning step automatically handles continuous covariates (parameter-instability test searches optimal cutpoint) multivariate interactions (later splits conditional earlier ones). assess sensitivity variable selection cutpoints particular sample, set stability = TRUE. Stability assessment. stability = TRUE, tree-fitting call replayed stability_B resamples data. returned stabletree object reports, per covariate, proportion resamples selected split, (continuous covariates) empirical distribution cutpoints. Stability independent effect-size classification – stable split negligible effect still negligible, large effect unstable split interpreted care. Cost roughly stability_B times original fitting time. Acknowledgement. effect-size machinery – MH partial gamma per node, iterative purification, ETS /B/C classification – follows implementations Henninger & Radek GitHub packages raschtreeMH effecttree. relevant code adapted MIT licence (see file header).

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMdimMartinLof
text: routine screening tool. test requires priori partition items subscales; using post-hoc , e.g., partition suggested RMdimResidualPCA()'s PC1 sign inflate Type-error rate. source papers state explicitly. Test statistic. items partitioned D subscales, total score \(t\) subscores \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\) (Christensen et al. 2002, eq. 22): $$T = 2\Bigl[\sum_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}   n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}\log(n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}/N)   - \sum_t n_t\log(n_t/N)   - \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}) + \sum_d \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)})\Bigr]$$ \(\ell_C\) conditional log-likelihood \(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)}\) CML estimates d-th subscale alone. CML fits use psychotools::raschmodel() (RM) psychotools::pcmodel() (PCM) speed. Monte Carlo sampling H0. Following Christensen & Kreiner (2007): () sample N total scores empirical score distribution \(n_t/N\); (b) sampled score, sample item-response vector conditional distribution \(p(x \mid t, \hat{\epsilon})\) given eq. 4 paper. dichotomous data fast algorithm Christensen & Kreiner (2007, p. 23) used (sample without replacement weighted item easinesses). polytomous data recursive \(\gamma\)-function approach used, item's response sampled conditional remaining items' joint score distribution (computed via psychotools::elementary_symmetric_functions()). Iterations fail (e.g., simulated dataset empty category item) silently dropped. Item parameters estimated observed data held fixed across MC iterations. Christensen & Kreiner (2007) use extended likelihood function (Tjur, 1982) empirical score distribution non-parametric estimate latent distribution, distributional assumption \(\theta\) needed.

text: cell joint subscore table (indexed \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\)), conditional probability H0 given total score \(t = \sum_d t_d\) $$p(t_1, \ldots, t_D \mid t) = \prod_d \gamma^{(d)}_{t_d} / \gamma_t,$$ expected count \(e = n_t \cdot p\), residual \((o - e) / \sqrt{n_t \cdot p \cdot (1 - p)}\). CML estimates unidimensional model used \(\gamma\)-functions. Reading table (D = 2): unidimensional null, residuals patternless roughly N(0, 1). Multidimensionality positively correlated dimensions typically shows positive residuals corners antidiagonal (high t1 + low t2, low t1 + high t2) negative residuals near antidiagonal centre (matched subscores). Negatively correlated dimensions show positive residuals table corners (high/low low/high) negative residuals high/high low/low. See Christensen et al. (2002, section7) worked example. title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed, least one complete case (row NA) must present. cutoff Optional. Default NULL (cutoff applied, behaviour identical current version). Can : return value RMitemInfitCutoff (list $item_cutoffs): data.frame extracted automatically number simulation iterations, cutoff_method, hdci_width included kable caption. $item_cutoffs data.frame RMitemInfitCutoff directly: must columns Item, infit_low, infit_high. provided, adds columns Infit_low, Infit_high, Flagged result. Default FALSE, case behaviour unchanged. correction Character. Multiple-comparison correction applied across items p_value = TRUE: "fwer" (default) Westfall-Young studentised-max step-(family-wise error rate), "fdr_bh" / "fdr_by" Benjamini-Hochberg / Benjamini-Yekutieli false discovery rate control, "none" uncorrected per-item p-values. alpha Numeric (0, 1). Significance level Flagged column p_value = TRUE (item flagged corrected p-value alpha). Default 0.05. output Character string controlling return value. Either "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame. sort Optional character string. sort = "infit", rows sorted Infit_MSQ descending order output.

Flagged character column values "overfit", "underfit", "" (previous logical). p_value = TRUE, columns p_infit (marginal two-sided p-value) padj_infit (corrected p-value) added Flagged reflects items padj_infit < alpha (direction Infit_MSQ relative 1).

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: Infit MSQ weighted fit statistic emphasises deviations near item location. Values close 1.0 indicate good fit. Values substantially 1.0 suggest underfit (unexpected responses), values substantially 1.0 suggest overfit (overly predictable responses). definition "substantially" depends several factors sample size, needs determined simulation using RMitemInfitCutoff. general rule--thumb value correct. Conditional infit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(), uses conditional distribution sufficient statistics (Müller, 2020). complete cases (rows without NA) used conditional fit calculation. Item parameters estimated conditional maximum likelihood via psychotools::pcmodel() (dichotomous item 2-category PCM); conditional infit/outfit MSQ comes iarm::out_infit() invariant estimation engine. Per-item average locations means CML thresholds, person-location reference mean Warm WLE estimates. Relative item location defined item's average location minus sample mean person location, providing measure item targeting. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports). Bootstrap p-values. p_value = TRUE, item's observed infit compared simulated null distribution (cutoff$results). per-item statistic residual studentised bootstrap mean SD – deliberately empirical SD rather Wilson-Hilferty / ZSTD transform, uninformative conditional MSQ (Müller, 2020). marginal p-value two-sided Monte-Carlo p-value (1 + #{|t*| >= |t|}) / (B + 1). correction = "fwer" family-wise adjustment uses Westfall-Young studentised-max step-, exploits bootstrap dependence among items powerful Bonferroni/Holm (Ferreira, 2024); validity rests subset pivotality. "fdr_bh"/"fdr_by" apply Benjamini-Hochberg / Benjamini- Yekutieli instead. model-conditional, sample-size-sensitive p-values reported alongside simulated effect-size band, place . p-values can smaller 1 / (B + 1), studentised-max (FWER) correction liberal simulation small (bootstrap mean/SD used studentisation noisy). least 1000 iterations RMitemInfitCutoff() recommended – simulations family-wise error rate controlled nominal level – warning issued simulation smaller. marginal p-values well calibrated even hundred iterations.

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: marginal p-value controls error rate single comparison: one item (item pair) decided advance relevant value. scanning k comparisons flagging whichever fall alpha tests k hypotheses , chance least one false flag inflates roughly \(1 - (1 - \alpha)^k\) (e.g. 34% k = 8 alpha = 0.05) – even every marginal p-value correctly calibrated. corrected (adjusted) p-value controls : correction = "fwer" bounds probability false flag (strict, lower power), "fdr_bh" / "fdr_by" bound expected proportion false flags among raised (lenient middle ground). Rule thumb: use marginal p-value single pre-specified comparison, corrected p-value screening whole table – usual workflow.

Methods testing 'small' 'moderate' number hypotheses simultaneously. Journal Statistical Theory Practice, 19(6). doi:10.1007/s42519-024-00412-4 Westfall, P. H., & Young, S. S. (1993). code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("iarm", quietly = TRUE)) {   # Simulate binary item response data (5 items, 40 persons)   set.seed(42)   sim_data <- as.data.frame(     matrix(sample(0:1, 40 * 5, replace = TRUE), nrow = 40, ncol = 5)   )   colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:5)    # Default kable output   RMitemInfit(sim_data)    # Sorted by infit MSQ descending   RMitemInfit(sim_data, sort = "infit")    # Return as data.frame for further processing   df <- RMitemInfit(sim_data, output = "dataframe")    # Simulation-based cutoffs (100 Monte-Carlo iterations)   if (requireNamespace("ggdist", quietly = TRUE)) {     cutoff_res <- RMitemInfitCutoff(sim_data, iterations = 100,                                     parallel = FALSE, seed = 42)     RMitemInfit(sim_data, cutoff = cutoff_res)     RMitemInfit(sim_data, cutoff = cutoff_res, output = "dataframe")      # Bootstrap p-values with family-wise (Westfall-Young) correction     # (use iterations >= 1000 in real analyses for stable p-values)     RMitemInfit(sim_data, cutoff = cutoff_res, p_value = TRUE,                 output = "dataframe")   } } #> Warning: Bootstrap p-values are based on only 100 simulation iterations. title: Person Locations for a Rasch / Partial Credit Model — RMpersonParameters
text: output = "dataframe", data.frame one row per respondent (input order; respondents responses dropped message) columns theta (person location), sem (standard error measurement), sum_score, n_answered (number non-missing responses), extreme (logical: minimum maximum possible score given items answered). output = "kable", content knitr_kable object. output = "ggplot", histogram theta.

title: Person Locations for a Rasch / Partial Credit Model — RMpersonParameters
text: Item parameters obtained (CML MML, taken item_params) treated fixed. person's theta estimated items answered. WLE solves Warm's weighted-likelihood score equation l'(theta) + J(theta) / (2 (theta)) = 0 bracketed root finding, bias-correction term J / (2 ) keeps equation solvable boundaries. SEM 1 / sqrt((theta)). Unlike MLE, Warm's estimator therefore yields finite locations minimum maximum possible scores (sensible step beyond next-extreme score rather +/-Inf; cf. Warm, 1989), matching established implementations catR TAM. scores still marked extreme column extrapolated carry large standard errors. root lies outside theta_range boundary returned NA SEM. EAP integrates pattern likelihood normal prior quadrature grid; estimate posterior mean SEM posterior standard deviation. Unlike WLE, EAP finite extreme scores prior shrinks inward, cost depending assumed prior. prior actually used – including marginal-ML estimate prior_sd left NULL – reported kable caption attached result attr(result, "prior").

what: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Computes three reliability indices Rasch / partial credit model: Person Separation Index (PSI) – WLE-based separation reliability – marginal reliability (native, CML test information integrated estimated normal latent density), Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) via RMUreliability() applied plausible values mirt::fscores().

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
code: RMreliability(   data,   conf_int = 0.95,   draws = 1000,   rmu_iter = 50,   estim = "WLE",   boot = FALSE,   boot_iter = 200,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   theta_range = c(-10, 10),   output = "kable" )

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). conf_int Numeric (0, 1). HDCI width bootstrap CIs RMU. Default 0.95. draws Integer. Number plausible-value draws drawn mirt model RMU calculation. Default 1000. gives stable RMU; computational cost mostly linear. rmu_iter Integer. Number times RMUreliability() repeated set plausible-value draws (repetition uses fresh random column split). Estimates averaged across repetitions stabilise split-induced variability. Default 50. estim Character. Theta estimator used mirt::fscores() RMU plausible-value seed. One "WLE" (default), "EAP", "MAP", "ML". Plausible draws produced Metropolis-Hastings. (PSI marginal reliability computed natively use .) boot Logical. TRUE, run non-parametric bootstrap obtain CIs PSI Marginal reliability. Default FALSE. boot_iter Integer. Number bootstrap iterations boot = TRUE. Default 200. parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai bootstrap available. Default TRUE. n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first; neither set, bootstrapping falls back sequential. seed Integer NULL. Master random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical. Print progress messages progress bar bootstrap. Default FALSE. theta_range Numeric length-2 vector. Theta limits passed mirt::fscores(). Default c(-10, 10). output Character. "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: output = "kable": knitr_kable object one row per metric. output = "dataframe": data.frame columns metric, estimate, lower, upper, notes.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Confidence intervals PSI Marginal reliability obtained non-parametric bootstrap (resampling respondents; three indices recomputed natively per resample, model refitted mirt). RMU interval HDCI correlations across plausible-value draws, averaged rmu_iter random splits draws. Marginal reliability native Green (1984) coefficient, \(1 - \overline{1/(\theta)}/\sigma^2\), test information \((\theta)\) summed CML item parameters average error variance taken estimated normal latent density \(N(0, \sigma^2)\) (\(\sigma\) marginal ML). Unlike mirt::marginal_rxx(), assumes \(N(0,1)\), integrates estimated latent variance, correct Rasch logit scale (\(\sigma\) typically well 1, \(N(0,1)\) assumption underestimates reliability). model-based complement sample-based PSI; large gap two flags -target non-normal sample. PSI WLE-based separation reliability, \(1 - \overline{SEM^2} / \mathrm{Var}(\hat\theta)\), computed CML item thresholds (psychotools) Warm's WLE person locations / analytic SEMs. Respondents extreme (min/max) raw scores excluded – boundary estimates inflate person variance overstate reliability. (Earlier versions used eRm::SepRel() MLE; values can differ, noticeably scales many extreme scorers, e.g. dichotomous items.) RMU Bignardi, Kievit, & Bürkner (2025), modified use mirt plausible values rather fully Bayesian posterior draws (see Mislevy, 1991, plausible-values framework). Bootstrap iterations fail converge silently dropped.

D., Humphreys, L. G., Linn, R. L., & Reckase, M. D. (1984). Technical Guidelines Assessing Computerized Adaptive Tests. Journal Educational Measurement, 21(4), 347–360. doi:10.1111/j.1745-3984.1984.tb01039.x Mislevy, R. J. (1991). Randomization-Based Inference Latent Variables Complex Samples. title: Raw-Score to Logit Score Transformation Table — RMscoreSE
text: function automatically detects whether data dichotomous (max score 1) polytomous (max score > 1) selects appropriate Rasch / Partial Credit model. method = "WLE" fits model CML psychotools::pcmodel(), centres item thresholds grand-mean-zero, solves Warm's weighted-likelihood equation raw score engine used RMpersonParameters(); two functions therefore report identical locations standard errors. Warm's bias correction yields finite locations even minimum maximum scores (root outside theta_range clamped boundary NA SE). reported logit_se information-based standard error 1 / sqrt((theta)), matching catR, TAM Rasch software. method = "EAP" fits model mirt::mirt(..., itemtype = "Rasch") (MML) obtains sum-score-based EAP estimates posterior SDs via mirt::fscores(method = "EAPsum", full.scores = FALSE, full.scores.SE = TRUE). EAP estimates finite score boundaries (prior shrinks inward), depend assumed normal prior theta. Item parameters MML differ slightly CML values used WLE path; well-behaved data difference small.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed. robust Logical. FALSE (default), histogram annotations use mean ± SD. TRUE, median ± MAD used instead. sort_items Character string controlling item ordering y-axis bottom panel. "data" (default) preserves column order data (first item top). "location" sorts items average threshold location (easiest top, hardest bottom). bins Integer. Number bins histograms. Default number unique scores divided 2, less 11. xlim Numeric vector length 2. Initial lower upper limits shared x-axis. Automatically expanded person item threshold values fall outside limits. ci_level Numeric. Confidence level item threshold error bars. Default 0.95 (95% CI). Set NULL hide error bars. person_fill Fill colour person histogram. Default "#0072B2" (blue). threshold_fill Fill colour item threshold histogram. Default "#D55E00" (vermillion). height_ratios Numeric vector length 3 specifying relative heights top (person), middle (threshold), bottom (dot-whisker) panels. Default c(3, 2, 5). output Character string. "patchwork" (default) returns combined patchwork plot. "list" returns named list three ggplot objects (p1, p2, p3) customisation.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: output = "patchwork": patchwork object (combined ggplot). output = "list": named list elements p1 (person histogram), p2 (threshold histogram), p3 (item threshold dot-whisker plot).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: Together, top middle panels form back--back histogram makes easy assess whether test well-targeted sample. Estimation method selection. function checks whether item response category fewer 3 observations. categories least 3 responses, item threshold locations standard errors estimated via Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) using psychotools::pcmodel() (dichotomous item 2-category PCM). category fewer 3 responses, function falls back Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) estimation via mirt::mirt() itemtype = "Rasch" SE = TRUE, numerically stable sparse-category conditions. message emitted MML fallback used. cases, item threshold locations centered (shifted grand mean thresholds equals zero). Person estimates obtained Warm's weighted likelihood (WLE) fitted item thresholds, consistent rest package. WLE finite extreme scores, -zero perfect responders located rather dropped. Confidence intervals item thresholds based Wald-type intervals: threshold estimate ± z × SE, z standard normal quantile corresponding ci_level. ggplot2 patchwork packages must installed (Suggests, Imports).

what: easyRasch2: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory — easyRasch2-package
title: easyRasch2: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory — easyRasch2-package
text: Streamlines reproducible Rasch measurement theory analyses ordinal item-response data, combining estimation routines 'eRm', 'psychotools', 'mirt', 'iarm', 'lavaan' consistent diagnostic, plotting, reporting layers. Covers four basic psychometric criteria summarised Christensen et al. (2021) doi:10.1111/sms.13908  – unidimensionality, local independence, ordered response category thresholds, invariance across subgroups – together item fit, targeting, reliability, category functioning, descriptive item-response plots. distinguishing feature use simulation-based critical values replace rule--thumb cutoffs conditional infit mean-square, Yen's Q3 local-dependence statistic, largest residual-PCA eigenvalue, ordinal CFA fit indices, partial-gamma DIF local-dependence coefficients, optionally augmented multiplicity-corrected bootstrap p-values. Outputs knitr::kable() tables 'ggplot2' figures suitable direct inclusion 'Quarto' 'R Markdown' reports.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMdimMartinLof() / RMdimMartinLofResiduals(): corrected category weights Monte Carlo null sampler expected-count computation (present 1.0.0). item parameters passed psychotools::elementary_symmetric_functions() wrong sign convention (weights categories exp(-par)), gamma functions used sign-inverted weights sampler’s numerator used correct ones. Consequences bug: simulated null datasets follow fitted model, Monte Carlo null distribution (hence p-value) depended systematically column order data, residual table’s expected counts wrong. fixed sampler reproduces exact conditional pattern distribution (verified brute-force enumeration), expected counts match enumeration exactly. Martin-Löf p-values residuals change relative 1.0.0 — typically corrected null distribution sits lower, rejections become somewhat clearer. addition, items now processed fixed (alphabetical) internal order, seed reproduces p-value regardless data’s columns arranged.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMdimMartinLof() now returns p_value_floor (1 / (actual_iterations + 1)), smallest attainable Monte Carlo p-value; documentation explains p-value equal floor means simulated statistic reached observed one read “p < floor” (increase iterations finer resolution). vignette’s Martin-Löf example now sets seed discusses . RMdifTree() gains output = "list", returning tidy effect-size table ($table) augmented tree object ($tree) single fit (used jamovi module). RMpersonFit() gains output = "list", returning per-person data.frame ($fit) named list person-fit maps ($plots) single computation — avoiding second resampling run views needed (used jamovi module). output = "dataframe" now returns unrounded values across package; rounding presentation concern now happens rendering kable (whose displayed precision unchanged). Affected functions: RMitemInfit(), RMitemInfitMI(), RMitemRestscore(), RMitemRestscoreBoot(), RMlocdepQ3() ($pairs table), RMdifGamma(), RMlocdepGamma(), RMdimResidualPCA(), RMdimCFA() ($fit $loadings), RMreliability(), RMdifLR(), RMdifTree() (including stability summary attribute), RMdimMartinLof() (wle_correlation element), RMdimMartinLofResiduals(), RMpersonParameters() (dataframe file/CSV output), RMpersonFit(), RMitemParameters() (dataframe file/CSV output; previously rounded 4 decimals, also made wide format’s mean location differ mean rounded threshold columns 5th decimal). RMscoreSE() already followed convention. Consequently, Flagged labels sorting now always computed exact (unrounded) values. Items pairs sitting exactly rounded boundary principle change flag relative earlier releases; displayed tables otherwise identical. Deliberately unchanged: infit simulation workers still round per-iteration statistics 3 decimals cutoff computation, simulation-based expected ranges reproduce earlier releases exactly. Minor layout changes RMtargeting() regarding number bins set user. Earlier sum score + 1, now sum score divided 2. RMitemICCPlot() gets slightly larger points thicker error bars.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMitemICCPlot() gains two grouping methods observed means, alongside existing quantile grouping (method = "cut" now legacy alias method = "quantile"): method = "width" equal-width intervals total-score scale, method = "manual" new score_breaks argument – total scores new group starts (concept RASCHplot’s lower.groups, without leading zero). documentation grouping rules error_band (model-implied interval observed mean per total score) substantially expanded. RMplotTile() gains text_size argument cell labels (default 4, matching RMitemCatProb()), cells zero responses now visually distinct: taken colour scale shown unfilled light grey outline (zero_fill = "white", set NULL previous behaviour), absence data reads absence rather darkest end fill scale, n = 0 nearly indistinguishable n = 1. caption notes convention empty cells present. RMlocdepGamma()’s output = "dataframe" tables now include se, lower, upper (95% Wald CI) columns pair, matching RMdifGamma(); formatted kable output unchanged.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMreliability() now fully reproducible seed: RMU estimate previously varied slightly identical calls mirt’s Metropolis-Hastings plausible-value sampler leaves R random-number stream nondeterministic state (draws reproducible), perturbed RMU split-half column assignments downstream. function now re-seeds RMU iterations. Respondents responses (-NA rows) longer crash functions fit CML model data retaining missing values. psychotools::pcmodel() errors -NA rows opaque “invalid argument type” (psychotools::raschmodel() segfaults), rows now dropped front — standard “N respondent(s) responses dropped.” message — RMlocdepQ3Cutoff(), RMitemInfit(), RMitemRestscore(), RMitemRestscoreBoot(), RMreliability(), observed-data overlays RMlocdepQ3Plot() RMitemInfitPlot(). RMdifLR() now drops rows (jointly dif_var) instead failing eRm::LRtest()’s error. Functions already used complete cases called .drop_empty_respondents() unaffected. RMlocdepQ3Cutoff()’s sample_n now counts respondents actually used (-NA rows excluded, incomplete responses still retained) sample_n_total raw input rows, matching RMlocdepQ3() *Cutoff() objects; previously two documented always equal. Captions affected functions keep reporting raw total n = X Y respondents (policy) form.

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-05 large consolidation release. headline change whole package now runs single estimation engine — conditional maximum likelihood (CML) item parameters via psychotools Warm’s weighted-likelihood (WLE) person locations — replacing previous mix eRm (MLE) mirt (MML/EAP). top : new CFA loading diagnostics, three new functions person+item parameters person fit, bootstrap p-values multiplicity correction, round naming/output consistency fixes. release eRm used RMdifLR() (Andersen’s LR test) mirt RMU plausible values (RMitemInfitPlot()’s observed-fit overlay moved eRm fit psychotools::pcmodel(), matching RMitemInfitCutoff(); values agree ~1e-6). Accordingly, eRm moved Imports Suggests: RMdifLR() errors install hint absent. Many items shift reported numbers slightly: underlying statistics unchanged, estimation engine (WLE locations finite extreme scores, extreme-score cases now retained rather dropped).

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: Observed item statistics — RMitemRestscore(), RMitemInfit(), RMitemCatProb(), RMitemHierarchy(), RMtargeting() — moved CML (psychotools) + WLE. conditional iarm statistics engine-invariant; minor things move common grand-mean-zero scale (Relative_location via WLE person mean; threshold SEs ~%; polytomous ICC curve position; targeting person distribution). RMdifLR() deliberately stays eRm::LRtest(). Parameter / score functions — shared .rasch_fit_cml() behind RMitemParameters(), RMpersonParameters(), RMscoreSE() now fits CML via psychotools. Locations, person estimates score SEs unchanged (match eRm ~5e-5); RMitemParameters()’s threshold SEs shift slightly (psychotools vcov vs eRm delta method, ~%). RMscoreSE() — WLE method now reports information-based SE 1/sqrt((theta)) (matching RMpersonParameters(), catR, TAM); point estimates unchanged, SEs differ (larger) score extremes, WLE solver/centring now shared RMpersonParameters(). RMreliability() — empirical reliability replaced native marginal reliability (Green 1984; “Empirical” row now “Marginal”), mirt::marginal_rxx() uses N(0,1), integrates estimated latent variance, correct Rasch logit scale; PSI now WLE-based separation reliability (1 - mean(SEM^2)/Var(theta), excluding extreme scorers), replacing eRm::SepRel() + mirt. bootstrap now fully native (mirt per resample). PSI can differ noticeably scales many extreme scorers; alpha RMU unchanged. Simulation-based tools now generate refit CML/WLE engine: RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Cutoff(), RMitemInfitCutoff(), RMitemInfitMI()/RMitemInfitCutoffMI(), RMitemRestscoreBoot(), RMdimResidualPCA()/RMdimResidualPCACutoff(), RMdimCFACutoff(), RMdimMartinLof(), RMdifGammaCutoff(), RMlocdepGammaCutoff(). eRm-based internal extract_item_thresholds() helper removed. Conditional / scale-invariant statistics (infit/outfit, item-restscore classification, Martin-Löf Monte-Carlo p-value, pooled MI infit MSQ/SE) unchanged; residual-based simulated quantities shift slightly — Q3 values/cut-offs/flags (statistic unchanged; observed-vs-old-MML correlation typically >0.95), PCA eigenvalues / variance partition / cut-, gamma cut-offs. RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Cutoff() keep previous engine available via estimator = "MML" (default "CML"); estimator stored cutoff object reused RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Plot() (mismatch overridden warning). RMitemInfitMI()’s pooled-SE caveat (Müller 2020) now documented.

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: RMpersonFit() — per-respondent conditional infit/outfit MSQ standardized log-likelihood lz, computed per response pattern (handles partial missingness, biased person estimate residual). Significance Monte-Carlo resampling fitted model, unreliable asymptotic null (Sinharay 2016; Müller 2020). Output table, data.frame, named list person-fit maps; zstd = TRUE adds Wilson-Hilferty transform (comparability , inference); flag = "underfit" restricts flagging validity-relevant direction. RMitemParameters() — item difficulty (dichotomous) Andrich-threshold (polytomous) parameters long/wide format, optional SEs Wald CIs. RMpersonParameters() — per-respondent theta SEM computed response pattern (handles partial missingness), via Warm’s WLE (default) EAP normal prior (SD estimated marginal ML unless fixed). RMitemParameters() RMpersonParameters() support output = "file" write result table CSV filename (data.frame also returned invisibly).

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: CFA now also reports per-item standardized factor loadings (observed vs simulated expected range), restructured match simulation tools: RMdimCFACutoff() now simulate-— returns simulation object (null distributions + cutoffs fit indices loadings); longer computes observed fit, output = "kable" errors pointer RMdimCFA(). (Breaking.) RMdimCFA() (new) takes data + required cutoff object returns list two tables, $fit (CFI/RMSEA/SRMR) $loadings (observed loading vs two-sided expected range), kables (default) data.frames. RMdimCFAPlot(cutoff_res, data) now returns list two ggplots ($loadings, RMitemInfitCutoffPlot() style; $fit, faceted fit-index distributions) requires data. (Breaking: previously single ggplot.)

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: Six simulation-based diagnostics gain optional bootstrap p-values computed simulated null distributions (p_value = TRUE; default FALSE leaves existing output unchanged). Shared mechanics: statistic studentised bootstrap mean/SD; marginal Monte-Carlo p-values floor 1/(B+1); correction offers Westfall-Young studentised-max step-FWER (default), Benjamini-Hochberg Benjamini-Yekutieli FDR, "none"; Flagged reflects padj < alpha simulated effect-size bands stay tables. full *Cutoff() object required, >= 1000 cutoff iterations recommended (warning ). (Ferreira 2024; Westfall & Young 1993.) Per function: RMdifGamma(): two-sided per item. asymptotic BH-adjusted p-value star columns iarm dropped mode (one p-value family per table); p_gamma / padj_gamma replace . RMdimCFA(): one-sided unfavourable direction CFI / RMSEA / SRMR, two-sided per-item loadings, corrected two separate families. RMdimResidualPCA(): single one-sided test first-contrast eigenvalue, multiplicity correction involved. RMitemInfit(): two-sided per item. RMlocdepGamma(): one-sided per pair excess positive LD (matching RMlocdepQ3()), computed per pair canonical direction (rest score = total − Item2, simulated direction) repeated direction-2 table; correction runs full pair family n_pairs display filter; iarm BH columns dropped RMdifGamma(). RMlocdepQ3(): one-sided per pair, folded new per-pair $pairs table returned alongside $matrix (observed Q3 vs simulated band, directional flag, sorted departure per-pair median, optional n_pairs cap). RMitemInfit(), RMlocdepQ3(), newly extended functions share “Multiple comparisons” help section.

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: table plot captions now report estimation sample size common form: n = X Y respondents (<policy>), Y appears respondents excluded policy note (complete cases / incomplete responses retained / missing values imputed) input contained missing values — complete data reads simply n = X respondents. Terminology “respondents” throughout (previously mix “persons” / “complete cases”). Sample size newly reported RMitemParameters(), RMpersonParameters(), RMitemHierarchy(), RMitemCatProb(), RMscoreSE(), RMlocdepQ3(), RMdimResidualPCA(), RMpersonFit()’s kable, descriptive plots (RMplotBar(), RMplotStackedbar(), RMplotTile()); captions reworded common form. bootstrap-null plots (RMitemInfitPlot(), RMdifGammaPlot(), RMlocdepGammaPlot(), RMlocdepQ3Plot(), RMdimCFAPlot()) report simulation sample way, suffixed per dataset; *Cutoff() objects store sample_n_total / sample_has_na, graceful fallback cutoff objects older versions. Respondents responses (-NA rows) dropped one-time message rather triggering CML fitting error, RMplotTile() likewise messages rows NA group dropped (previously silent; item-level NAs retained descriptive plots). RMdifGammaCutoff() longer prints iarm::partgam_DIF() result table every iteration sequential (parallel = FALSE) runs; silencing sink now also restored safely iteration failure (hardened RMlocdepGammaCutoff() ). RMdifLR() captions now report Andersen LR p-value plain number (exact three decimals, p < 0.001 ) instead format.pval() scientific notation (<1e-04), test statistic shown χ² instead chi^2. Naming aligned across functions (deprecation aliases): RMdimResidualPCA() plot output now output = "ggplot" ("loadings" kept backward-compatible alias); RMtargeting() output = "figure" → "patchwork" (matching RMitemICCPlot()); RMitemRestscore() p.adj → p_adj; RMitemInfitPlot() output → statistic. RMitemInfitCutoffPlot() renamed RMitemInfitPlot(), matching <base>Plot form bootstrap-cutoff plot functions (RMdifGammaPlot(), RMlocdepGammaPlot(), RMlocdepQ3Plot(), RMdimCFAPlot()). old name shipped 0.8.0 CRAN release, kept deprecated alias warns forwards (unlike 0.8.0 renames, dropped old names outright). Separately, RMdimCFAPlot()’s first argument cutoff_res renamed simfit match plot functions (alias). RMdifLR() now defaults output = "kable" ("ggplot"), matching every function offers kable ggplot output (RMscoreSE(), RMdimMartinLof(), RMpersonFit(), RMpersonParameters(), RMdimResidualPCA()). Pass output = "ggplot" previous default. Flagged column now labels misfit direction ("overfit" / "underfit" / "") instead logical TRUE/FALSE RMitemInfit() / RMitemInfitMI() (column type changes logical character). RMitemRestscore() gains Flagged column drops redundant significance-star Location columns (trimmed headers + explanatory caption). Note value direction differs: high infit underfit, high restscore correlation overfit.

title: easyRasch2 1.0.0
text: RMitemInfitMI() RMitemInfitCutoffMI() now accept mids objects whose items imputed ordered factors (required mice’s polr method): completed data coerced back numeric responses internally, rather erroring factor columns. RMitemInfitCutoff() RMlocdepQ3Cutoff() gain experimental dgp argument — "resample" (default; resample WLE locations simulate, marginal null) vs "conditional" (simulate pattern exact Rasch conditional given observed total score, matched conditional null) — stored result. See dev/q3_dgp_comparison.R / dev/infit_dgp_comparison.R. Q3 plot table outputs now share $matrix / $pairs structure. RMlocdepQ3Plot() returns list two plots: $pairs (per-pair simulated-vs-observed dot-interval, previously single return value) $matrix (lower-triangle diverging-RdYlBu Q3 tile heatmap -cutoff pairs outlined, adapted RASCHplot::ggQ3star(); needs data, else NULL). RMitemICCPlot() reimplemented CML engine, replacing iarm::ICCplot() wrapper. conditional item curve variance now come psychotools CML thresholds + exact conditional distribution given total score. draws confidence intervals observed class-interval means (ci, default ) optional model band (error_band); DIF mode shows per-group CIs annotates panel partial-gamma DIF magnitude (iarm::partgam_DIF, RMdifGamma()), full table attached attr(., "dif_gamma"). New arguments ci, error_band, conf_level, min_n (per-cell floor, 8), items; method / class_intervals / dif_var / output unchanged. conditional-ICC approach follows Buchardt, Christensen & Jensen (2023) RASCHplot package. RMlocdepGamma(): corrected $direction2 caption / @return wording “total - Item2” (note direction 2 lists pairs reverse order); computations unchanged (labelling fix). RMdifTree(): fixed “variable lengths differ” error single covariate passed index expression (e.g. covariates = phq9[, 10]). derived non-syntactic column name now matched literal column rather re-evaluated R expression original (pre-NA-drop) data. RMdifTree(stability = TRUE) longer silences console front-ends (e.g. RStudio) redirect message stream. internal stderr capture used muffle resample-fit noise reset message sink default, clobbering front-end’s sink; now redirects foreign message sink active. RMitemRestscore(p_adj = "none") now returns unadjusted p-values instead NA “NAs introduced coercion” warning. adjustment iarm::item_restscore() omits adjusted-p column, p-value now selected name rather fixed column position (picked significance-stars column). table header reads “p-value” case.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-08

what: Visual consistency across all plot output
title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: er2_axis_margins() — extra breathing room around x y axis titles. er2_plot_caption() — left-aligned 9 pt plot caption. optional ggtext package installed (new Suggests), caption renders via ggtext::element_markdown() APA-conventional italic “Note.” prefix can sit alongside roman body text. Without ggtext, falls back plain element_text(face = "italic") plain “Note.” prefix — markdown asterisks ever leak rendered text. er2_caption() — builds caption string “Note.” prefix wraps long captions 90 characters via strwrap() longer run right edge plot. body text wrapped first prefixed, prefix never broken line break. three helpers internal (@noRd) exported.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: Optional label_curves = "path" mode (requires optional geomtextpath package, Suggests) writes category’s label along curve classic IRT trace-plot style. Single-item — pass full multi-item dataset use item argument choose item’s curves plot. model still fit items (CML threshold estimation needs full dataset); rendering filtered. category’s label positioned curve’s modal theta (peak bell middle categories; relevant edge monotone extreme categories), clamped small margin plot edges prevent clipping.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: release renames 22 exported functions consistent domain-prefix → method → variant-suffix scheme autocompletion prefix (RMdif, RMlocdep, RMitem, RMdim, RMplot) surfaces every related function. semantic changes; names. complete mapping new ?easyRasch2-renaming help page. short version: Unchanged: RMreliability(), RMUreliability(), RMtargeting(), RMscoreSE(). Breaking change. deprecation aliases shipped — existing scripts need search--replace. See ?easyRasch2-renaming full table.

title: easyRasch2 0.7.1
text: New RMlocdepQ3plot() visualising per-pair simulated Q3 distributions observed Q3 values, mirroring design RMpgLDplot() RMpgDIFplot(). Supports items n_pairs filters; data supplied, ranks n_pairs |observed Q3 - median(simulated Q3 per pair)|. RMlocdepQ3cutoff() now retains per-pair iteration-level data: returned list gains pair_results, pair_cutoffs, item_names, cutoff_method, hdci_width. Existing scalar outputs (suggested_cutoff etc.) unchanged. New arguments cutoff_method ("hdci" / "quantile") hdci_width (default 0.99) control per-pair credible intervals computed. RMlocdepQ3() can now called full list returned RMlocdepQ3cutoff() (auto-extracts $suggested_cutoff), matching partial-gamma family convention. Numeric scalar still works. RMpartgamLD() RMpgLDplot() gain n_pairs argument keep top-N pairs |gamma| (LD) |observed - median(sim)| deviation (plot). RMpartgamLD(), RMpartgamDIF(), RMitemrestscore() now use consistent column headers: Adj. p-value (BH) new p-value sign. star-string column. RMitemrestscore()’s former Absolute_difference column now signed Difference (observed − expected), - underfit visually distinguishable glance.

title: easyRasch2 0.7.0
text: New RMbarplot() RMstackedbarplot() join RMtileplot() easy visualization item response data distributions. New RMciccPlot() conditional item characteristic curves plot, also includes DIF analysis categorical DIF variables. New RMitemHierarchy(), outputs plot illustrating item hierarchy item thresholds confidence intervals. Modified RMcfaCutoff() use .scaled metrics RMSEA CFI, stability. See documentation details. Bug fix RMmartinLof() dichotomous items. Bug fix RMitemHierarchy() making option item_labels work intended. Tests written functions, preparing CRAN submission nearish future. Drafted package intro/vignette

title: easyRasch2 0.6.0
text: RMcfaPlot() shows figure distribution simulation results observed model fit values.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.8
text: assess stability tree-based results. raschtreeMH (https://github.com/mirka-henninger/raschtreeMH) effecttree (https://github.com/mirka-henninger/effecttree) Henninger, M., Debelak, R., & Strobl, C. (2023). new stopping criterion Rasch trees based Mantel-Haenszel effect size measure DIF. Educational Psychological Measurement, 83, 181-212. doi:10.1177/00131644221077135 Henninger, M., Radek, J., Debelak, R., & Strobl, C. (2025). Partial credit trees meet partial gamma coefficient quantifying DIF DSF polytomous items. Behaviormetrika, 52, 221-257.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.7
text: New RMdifLR() testing DIF categorical variables using Andersens’s Likelihood Ratio test implemented package eRm Optional group faceting, useful DIF-analyses. Also added post-processing function RMmartinLofresiduals().

title: easyRasch2 0.5.6
text: Added RMresidualPCA() evaluating patterns standardized residuals RM/PCM. Outputs either table eigenvalues explained variance figure standardized loadings first residual contrast item locations. Added RMpcaCutoff() determine simulation-based critical value largest eigenvalue.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.5
text: Added RMbootRestscore() use large sample sizes. See https://pgmj.github.io/rasch_itemfit/ details. Added RMscoreSE() produces transformation table (figure) ordinal sum scores WLE (Weighted Likelihood Estimation) interval scores. Modified RMlocdepQ3cutoff() improve speed.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.4
text: RMtargeting() Wright map style plots. RMinfitcutoff_mi() uses mids object containing multiple imputated datasets (output mice::mice()) run simulations datasets combines results. Splits iterations across imputations (e.g. 250 total / 5 imputations = 50 ). RMiteminfit_mi() calculates pools conditional infit imputated datasets optionally uses RMinfitcutoff_mi() output cutoff values. Works existing RMinfitcutoffPlot()

title: easyRasch2 0.5.3
text: Plot function RMpgLDplot(), similar RMpgDIFplot() NOTE: simulation-based cutoffs yet evaluated systematic way. Default choices may sensible, please try different numbers iterations hdci_width.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.2
text: Also plot function RMpgDIFplot(), similar RMinfitcutoffPlot() NOTE: simulation-based cutoffs yet evaluated systematic way. Default choices may sensible, please try different numbers iterations hdci_width.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.1
text: New function RMinfitcutoffPlot() illustrate distribution simulated conditional item infit MSQ values together observed value.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.0
text: RMinfitcutoff() gains cutoff_method hdci_width parameters: default (cutoff_method = "hdci", hdci_width = 0.999), per-item cutoff intervals now computed using Highest Density Interval via ggdist::hdci() (99.9% HDCI). Set cutoff_method = "quantile" restore previous behaviour (2.5th/97.5th percentiles). ggdist package required cutoff_method = "hdci" (added Suggests). returned list now also includes cutoff_method hdci_width fields. RMiteminfit() caption updated: cutoff return value RMinfitcutoff(), kable caption now states cutoff method, e.g. "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI)." "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (2.5th/97.5th percentile).".

title: easyRasch2 0.4.0
text: RMiteminfit() gains optional cutoff parameter: Accepts return value RMinfitcutoff() ($item_cutoffs data.frame directly). provided, per-item cutoff boundaries (Infit_low, Infit_high) logical Flagged column added "dataframe" "kable" output. kable caption includes number simulation iterations available. New RMinfitcutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination [RMiteminfit()]. Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). Returns per-item 2.5th 97.5th percentiles simulated infit outfit MSQ distributions ($item_cutoffs), together full iteration-level results ($results). Requires iarm package (Suggests).

title: easyRasch2 0.3.0
text: New RMiteminfit(): Computes conditional infit MSQ statistics item via iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "infit" sorts infit MSQ descending. complete cases used conditional fit calculation. New RMitemrestscore(): Computes observed model-expected item-restscore correlations via iarm::item_restscore(), enriched absolute differences observed expected values, item average locations, item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "diff" sorts absolute difference descending.

title: easyRasch2 0.2.0
text: RMlocdepQ3(): cutoff now optional (default NULL). omitted, raw Q3 residual correlation matrix returned without dynamic cut-applied. provided, dynamic cut-(mean Q3 + cutoff) shown kable caption . Supersedes requirement always supply cutoff value (PRs #2 #3). New RMlocdepQ3cutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination RMlocdepQ3(). Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). $suggested_cutoff 99th percentile simulated Q3 max–mean distribution. New internal helpers R/utils-simulation.R: sim_poly_item(), sim_partial_score(), extract_item_thresholds(). Dependencies: eRm, psychotools (>= 0.7-3), parallel, utils moved/added Imports. mirai added Suggests.

title: easyRasch2 0.1.0
text: Initial package structure. Added RMlocdepQ3() Yen’s Q3 residual correlation analysis local dependence.
